title: gilia viscida
intro: Gilia viscida, also known as sticky gilia, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Gilia viscida can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats.
